personas.subtitle: Unprompted control condition
personas.when_to_use: Use as the no-persona anchor for H2 comparisons.
personas.expected_effect: Expected to anchor the reference distribution while preserving prompt-presence asymmetry against prompted personas.
personas.risk_note: Baseline versus prompted-persona contrasts mix posture effects with prompt-presence effects.

personas.subtitle: Counter-argument and failure-mode pressure
personas.when_to_use: Use when you want models to test their own answer against objections before committing.
personas.expected_effect: Expected to widen the distribution by increasing cautious, qualified, or self-critical outputs.
personas.risk_note: May shift outputs toward caution or non-commitment.

personas.subtitle: Problem decomposition before synthesis
personas.when_to_use: Use when you want models to break complex questions into separable parts before answering.
personas.expected_effect: Expected to increase structured, assumption-sensitive answers and reduce leaps to conclusion.
personas.risk_note: May add structure without improving final answer quality.

personas.subtitle: Multiple plausible paths before narrowing
personas.when_to_use: Use when you want breadth before commitment on ambiguous questions.
personas.expected_effect: Expected to widen the distribution by increasing alternative framings or candidate conclusions.
personas.risk_note: May broaden the answer space and reduce decisiveness.

personas.subtitle: Single-best-answer commitment
personas.when_to_use: Use when you want models to converge on one answer under stated assumptions.
personas.expected_effect: Expected to tighten the distribution around clearer top-choice outputs.
personas.risk_note: May narrow the distribution by suppressing caveats or alternative paths.
